cards.title: Human Archive Pays Indian Gig Workers for Robot Training Data
cards.body: A UC Berkeley and Stanford-founded startup is paying gig workers in India to wear camera-equipped caps and sensors for collecting real-world physical training data. AI and robotics labs are racing to acquire this type of data for model development.

cards.title: Universal Music Group and TikTok Renew AI Music Combat Agreement
cards.body: UMG continues pushing platforms and AI companies to implement stricter content moderation policies. The renewed agreement aims to combat unauthorized AI music generation.

cards.title: ClickUp Replaces Hundreds of Employees with AI Agents
cards.body: The nine-year-old startup is undergoing mass layoffs while deploying thousands of AI agents. The shift signals broader workplace transformation trends in the industry.

cards.title: Google Navigates AI Security Challenges in Real Time
cards.body: Even major tech companies like Google are navigating AI security issues as they emerge. The industry is in a transition period for establishing best practices.

cards.title: Amazon's Bee Wearable Offers Convenience with Privacy Trade-offs
cards.body: Amazon's new AI wearable combines convenient features with privacy concerns similar to other AI wearables. The device represents growing tension between utility and user data collection.

cards.title: Ferrari Partners with IBM AI to Enhance Fan Experience
cards.body: Scuderia Ferrari HP and IBM are leveraging AI to redefine fan engagement in F1. The partnership shows how enterprise AI is transforming sports experiences.

cards.title: xAI Pivots to Natural Gas, Abandons Solar Focus
cards.body: Elon Musk's xAI has shifted away from solar power toward natural gas investments. SpaceX simultaneously pursues orbital data centers, diverging from Musk's previous 'solar-electric economy' vision.

cards.title: AI Used to Reconstruct Dead Pilots' Voices from Recordings
cards.body: Researchers used AI to reconstruct cockpit recordings from spectrograms, forcing the NTSB to temporarily block access to its docket system. The incident raises concerns about AI misuse in sensitive aviation data.

cards.title: Google Adds Disco Ball Icons to Pixel Home Screen
cards.body: Google is rolling out decorative disco ball icons for Pixel devices. The feature lets users customize their home screen with the playful design element.

cards.title: AI Startups Stretch Revenue Metrics While VCs Look Away
cards.body: Some AI startups are inflating traditional 'ARR' metrics when discussing progress publicly. Their investors are fully aware of these stretched accounting practices.

cards.title: SpaceX Files $1.75 Trillion IPO with Ambitious Mars Goals
cards.body: SpaceX's S-1 filing reveals a $28 trillion total addressable market and executive pay tied to Mars colony establishment. The 36-page risk factor section underscores the venture's scope and uncertainty.

cards.title: Google Search AI Update Breaks 'Disregard' Keyword Searches
cards.body: A Google Search AI update has caused the word 'disregard' to break the search interface. The glitch highlights ongoing issues with AI-powered search systems.

cards.title: Google Demos Android XR Glasses with Gemini AI Integration
cards.body: Google showcased prototype Android XR glasses that overlay Gemini-powered translation and navigation directly into users' field of view. The demonstration shows near-ready wearable AI technology.

cards.title: Spotify Partners with Universal for AI-Generated Music Covers
cards.body: Spotify is partnering with Universal Music Group to let Premium subscribers create AI-generated song covers and remixes. Participating artists receive revenue shares from the fan-created content.

cards.title: Six Search Engine Alternatives as Google Transforms
cards.body: Google's upcoming AI overview features are prompting users to explore alternative search engines. Several viable competitors now offer different approaches to web search.

cards.title: India's gig workers training the world's robots
cards.body: Human Archive, founded by UC Berkeley and Stanford researchers, pays Indian gig workers to collect real-world physical training data by wearing camera-equipped caps and sensors. This data is critical for AI and robotics labs racing to build more capable systems.

cards.title: UMG and TikTok renew AI music combat agreement
cards.body: Universal Music Group continues pushing platforms and AI companies to implement stricter content moderation policies. The renewed agreement aims to prevent unauthorized AI-generated music on TikTok.

cards.title: ClickUp replaces hundreds of workers with AI agents
cards.body: The nine-year-old startup is eliminating hundreds of employee positions by deploying thousands of AI agents. This shift signals how enterprise software is rapidly automating human roles.

cards.title: Pope's AI encyclical addresses power concentration
cards.body: Pope Leo XIV's encyclical uses AI as a lens to examine concentrated power and eroding democracy. The document critiques how a tech elite shapes the world to its advantage.

cards.title: Everyone navigating AI security in real time
cards.body: Even Google is working through AI security challenges as the industry experiences rapid development. The transition period requires all players to adapt security practices on the fly.

cards.title: Amazon's Bee wearable offers convenience and privacy anxiety
cards.body: Amazon's new AI wearable provides practical features alongside significant privacy concerns. Like other AI wearables, Bee exemplifies the tradeoff between convenience and data protection.

cards.title: Ferrari uses IBM's AI to create superfans
cards.body: IBM and Scuderia Ferrari are partnering to redefine the fan experience using AI technology. The collaboration explores how machine learning can deepen fan engagement in motorsports.

cards.title: AI reconstructs dead pilots' voices from recordings
cards.body: Researchers used AI spectrograms to reconstruct cockpit recordings from deceased pilots. The NTSB temporarily blocked access to its docket system in response to this unauthorized voice recreation.

cards.title: Google Search's AI breaks the word 'disregard'
cards.body: Google's AI-powered search update has inadvertently made searching for the word 'disregard' effectively impossible. The search interface breaks when processing this specific term.

cards.title: Google's AI glasses nearly ready for prime time
cards.body: Google demoed prototype Android XR glasses powered by Gemini that overlay translation, navigation, and information into your field of view. The demo showed the glasses are approaching practical usability.

cards.title: VCs and founders inflate AI startup 'ARR' metrics
cards.body: Some AI startups are stretching traditional revenue metrics when discussing progress publicly, with full awareness from their investors. The practice inflates perceived growth in the competitive AI funding landscape.

cards.title: Spotify and UMG allow fan-made AI covers
cards.body: Spotify is partnering with Universal Music Group to let Premium subscribers create AI-generated song covers and remixes. Participating artists will receive a share of the revenue generated.

cards.title: Six search engines worth trying now
cards.body: As Google integrates more AI features, alternative search engines are becoming viable options. Users unhappy with Google's AI overview feature have several compelling alternatives to explore.

cards.title: Rethinking org design for agentic AI era
cards.body: A disconnect exists between organizational ambitions for AI agents and current capabilities, with 85% wanting agentic systems within three years but 76% lacking supporting infrastructure. Enterprise AI adoption requires fundamental organizational restructuring.

cards.title: Reality check on AI job displacement panic
cards.body: Despite hysteria over AI's threat to white-collar jobs, aggregate employment remains stable with limited evidence of widespread displacement. A more nuanced picture is emerging beneath headline employment numbers.

cards.title: AI threatens entry-level work as aggregate jobs hold
cards.body: While overall employment has remained stable, AI threatens to disrupt entry-level positions where junior workers typically begin. This hidden crisis may reshape career progression more than headline job numbers suggest.

cards.title: Anthropic's Code with Claude shows coding future
cards.body: At Anthropic's London developer event, Code with Claude demonstrated how AI is transforming software development practices. The two-day conference explored the future of AI-assisted coding.

cards.title: Google I/O shows AI-driven science's new path
cards.body: Demis Hassabis of Google DeepMind stated we're in the 'foothills of the singularity' during Google I/O. Recent AI developments are reshaping the approach to AI-driven scientific research.

cards.title: Can AI learn to understand the world?
cards.body: AI companies are building systems to overcome LLM limitations and understand the external world. World models have become central to advancing AI capabilities beyond language processing.

cards.title: Scaling creativity in the age of AI
cards.body: Storytelling remains core to humanity, and technology continues reshaping both medium and distribution. AI is transforming how creative expression reaches audiences across formats.

cards.title: FBI explains identifying AI porn creators
cards.body: FBI agents reveal methods for identifying people posting non-consensual AI-generated pornography. Law enforcement is developing techniques to track down perpetrators of this emerging crime.

cards.title: 3D-printable humanoid legs enable robotics research
cards.body: Researchers have created 3D-printable humanoid legs to democratize robotics experimentation. The open-source design allows broader participation in robot development and testing.

cards.title: Pope cites Gandalf, urges disarming AI
cards.body: Pope Leo XIV references Tolkien's Gandalf while calling to 'disarm' AI systems. The religious leader emphasizes the need to control powerful AI technologies.

cards.title: US scrambles to stop recreating dead pilots
cards.body: The US government is taking action to prevent internet users from recreating deceased pilots' voices using AI. The practice of reconstructing historical audio raises significant ethical and legal concerns.
